generalRequirements.note: LAP must usually be valid for 3 months. LAP is not applicable for professionals, household employees, religious bodies, and sports sector cases. Those use a letter of accommodation instead.

generalRequirements.item: Letter of Authorisation + NIC of registered user

generalRequirements.note: Commercial partnerships or 'Societe' also need a Certificate of Posting. Household employees: not applicable.
If applicable: agency letter, director NIC, recruitment licence. Not needed for renewal.
Passport should have 6 months validity for new applications.
6 months validity. Not applicable for renewal.

generalRequirements.item: Vetted contract of employment or salary proof
generalRequirements.note: If salary is 50,000 MUR or below, contract vetting applies. If above 50,000 MUR, employer documentary evidence of salary is required.

notes: HEC recognition is required for academic qualifications before work permit application.
If the qualification is technical/vocational and not covered by HEC, recognition must be sought from MQA.

English and/or French literacy is mandatory for specific grades.
Most of these are N/A for renewal.

callQuestions: If LAP exists, total capacity and remaining capacity
If no lodging, note that housing search is needed near company location
If construction company, ask for CIA certificate
If very interested, escalate for Mr. Vayid follow-up

workflow.details: Record speaker name, date, call outcome, manpower need, nationality, local workers, expatriates, LAP status, and company location.
Confirm post / grade, number of expatriates, nationality, lodging situation, and whether escalation to Mr. Vayid is needed.
Check existing LAP capacity or start housing search near the company. Keep fire and sanitary clearance moving in parallel.
Contact agencies, request more CVs than final demand, and track all candidates in Excel.
Confirm interview dates and platform, send CV summaries, and schedule interviews candidate by candidate if needed.
Get final client choice, send confirmation email, and report update to the team.
Complete EIC vacancy follow-up where required and send verified report after team review.
Verify passport, photo, medical, and maintain one folder per candidate.
Prepare covering letter and arrange Ebène deposit.
Track both clearances in parallel until ready for LAP submission.
Submit LAP documents, pay processing fee and permit fee, then follow up on status.
Prepare contract documents, apply special Madagascar vetting if applicable, then submit work permit through EDB / NELS.
Follow up with PIO and agent for entry permit readiness and flight arrangements.
After arrival in Mauritius, follow up with PIO, pay applicable fee, and track residence permit issue date / validity.

documents: Health Clearance from Ministry of Health and Wellness / Quality of Life
Site plan and layout plan of employees' lodging accommodation
Owner authorisation + owner ID copy or lease agreement
Applicant ID copy if someone other than director is handling application
Director authorisation letter allowing the person to submit/amend the application
lap.renewal: Apply within 3 months before expiry, with fresh health and fire clearances. Permit lapses 30 days after expiry if not renewed. Late renewal may attract a 50% surcharge under the regulations.

rules: No employer may use a building as lodging accommodation without a permit.
Permit validity is up to 36 months.
Permit must be exhibited in a conspicuous place in the lodging accommodation.
Accommodation should not be in the same building as the factory or place of work, subject to transitional provisions.
Each room must have an updated lodger inventory sheet.
Bedroom floor area must be at least 4.6 m² for one employee with single bed, and 5.2 m² for any 2 employees with bunk beds.
Beds must be separate with proper mattresses. Minimum spacing is 1.1 m between single beds and 1.2 m where bunk beds are used.
Separate lodging accommodation areas must be provided for male and female employees.
Bedroom height must be at least 2.5 m.
Ventilation openings must be at least one tenth of floor area, with sufficient natural or artificial lighting.
Sanitary facilities must be accessible at all times, supplied with clean running water, and cleaned/disinfected every day.
Bathrooms and toilets need impervious non-skid floors; sanitary walls tiled to at least 2 m; bathroom walls tiled or waterproof-painted to at least 2 m.
Water storage tanks should be cleaned and disinfected at least every 6 months.
Kitchen must have adequate cooking facilities, refrigeration, air extraction, screened openings, grease trap drainage, separate scullery, and clean dining area.
Fire rules include emergency exits / escape provisions, extinguishers, warning system where applicable, and no smoking or cooking in bedrooms.
At least two socket outlets should be provided in each bedroom per lodger and no multiplug should be used in bedrooms.
A first-aid box and enough appointed persons / first-aiders should be available, with transport arrangements to hospital if needed.

waterClosets.overRule: For over 150, provide 1 additional sanitary convenience for every 25 male and for every 25 female employees.

washBasins.overRule: For over 125, provide 1 additional wash-basin for every 20 male or every 20 female employees.

bathrooms.overRule: For over 55, provide 1 additional bathroom for every 15 male or every 15 female employees.

remarksExamples.format: DD/MM/YYYY - Interested - Needs [number] expatriates for [post/grade] - Nationality: [x]

firstCallChecklist.text: Record name of speaker and date of call.
Note whether the outcome is no answer, not interested, or interested.
If interested, record number of expatriates needed, post / grade, and nationality required.
Ask how many local workers the company currently has.
Ask how many expatriates the company currently has.
Ask whether the company already has LAP.
If LAP exists, confirm permitted capacity and remaining available places.
If there is no lodging, note that housing search is required near the company location.
For construction companies only, ask whether the company has a CIA certificate.
If the client is very interested, note that Mr. Vayid should follow up directly.

interestedClientWorkflow: Confirm exact manpower need by post / grade and nationality.
Verify current local workers, current expatriates, and LAP status.
If lodging exists, check remaining capacity under the current LAP.
If no lodging exists, search accommodation near the company location.
Keep fire and sanitary clearance progress moving in parallel with lodging preparation.
If the client is highly interested, escalate for direct follow-up by Mr. Vayid.
candidateSourcing: Confirm post / grade and number of expatriates needed before contacting agencies.
Use the existing email templates to request candidates.
Request more CVs than the final number required so the team can compare profiles more effectively.
Example: if 5 candidates are needed, request around 10 CVs where possible.
Track agency, candidate, post, and CV status in the Excel database.
After internal review, share the updated candidate tracker with the team.
interviewProcess: Ask the client which dates they are available for interviews.
Confirm whether interviews will be held on WhatsApp or Teams.
If many candidates are involved, schedule them one by one.
Before interview, send the client a summary of each expatriate profile together with the CVs.
Schedule interview with client, expatriate, and internal coordinator.
If a WhatsApp group is created, remove candidates one by one after interview when no longer needed.
After interview, email the client to confirm which expatriate(s) they choose.
Send confirmation email and internal report after client selection.
eicProcess: Once potential expatriate candidates are confirmed, proceed with EIC follow-up where required.
Inform EIC that suitable candidates were not obtained locally and collect the vacancy form.
Send Director ID of Mr. Vayid as required.
Check email regularly for EIC follow-up.
Screen local candidates against the vacancy requirements and prepare the report.
Let the team verify the report before sending it to EIC.
candidateDocuments: Check whether the expatriate has a valid passport.
Request medical documents or arrange medical as required.
Create one folder per candidate and save passport, photo, medical, and related documents.
clearancesAndLap: Keep fire clearance and sanitary clearance moving in parallel.
Medical covering letter is deposited at Ebène by Mr. Vayid.
Fire clearance is handled in Port Louis.
Sanitary clearance is handled according to region.
Once medical, fire, and sanitary papers are ready, prepare the LAP submission file.
Pay the LAP processing fee and then the applicable LAP permit fee at Victoria House, Port Louis.
permitProcess: After clearances, start contract / COE preparation and work permit processing.
For Madagascar only, contract vetting / stamping is required.
For Nepal and India, no Madagascar vetting applies.
Submit work permit through EDB / NELS.
If there is no query, proceed with payment of the application / final claim as applicable.
Check status regularly, obtain the approved work permit, and send copy to the team.
Liaise with PIO for entry permit and later for residence permit after arrival.
Coordinate with the agent for flight tickets once entry documents are ready.
